x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces an OPEN H+H directory (facilities, service lines, providers, pharmacies, financial-assistance) that H+H does not publish today, plus read access to the patient's appointments (mirroring the live Epic FHIR Appointment read) and request_appointment — the net-new write workflow that falls outside the live FHIR surface (no Appointment create, no Slot, no $book).

server.name: io.nyc.hhc
server.title: NYC Health + Hospitals (H+H)
server.description: Agent-native access to NYC Health + Hospitals — an open directory of facilities (11 acute-care hospitals, Gotham Health federally qualified health centers, post-acute/nursing, behavioral-health, and Correctional Health), service lines, providers, pharmacies, and financial-assistance programs (NYC Care, sliding-scale), plus read access to a patient's appointments and the net-new ability to request an appointment. H+H already runs a live Epic FHIR R4 / SMART on FHIR standard, but it is read-mostly and patient-scoped with no open directory and no booking; this surface fills those gaps.
server.instructions: Use find_facilities / get_facility (and get_facility_services) to locate H+H care sites by borough, network (Acute Care, Gotham Health, etc.), facility type, or service offered. find_services covers clinical service lines; find_providers is Find a Doctor by specialty, language, or facility. find_financial_assistance surfaces NYC Care, the sliding-scale (Options) program, and financial counseling — H+H is the safety-net system, so route uninsured users here; eligibility is immigration-status-blind for NYC Care and the sliding scale. find_pharmacies lists outpatient pharmacies. list_my_appointments returns the authenticated patient's booked appointments (SMART on FHIR patient authorization required) — this maps to the live Epic FHIR Appointment read. request_appointment (net-new) creates an appointment REQUEST; it requires an authenticated patient and mutates state — confirm the service, facility, and patient details before submitting, set urgent=true for time-sensitive but non-emergency needs, and NEVER use it for emergencies (direct the user to 911 or an Emergency Department). This is a design-first proposal, not a booking into a real EHR.

tools.name: find_facilities
tools.title: Find Facilities
tools.description: Search NYC Health + Hospitals care sites by borough, network, facility type, emergency services, service offered, and/or free text.

tools.name: get_facility
tools.title: Get Facility
tools.description: Fetch a single H+H facility (network, type, services, hours, location) by id.

tools.name: get_facility_services
tools.title: Get Facility Services
tools.description: List the clinical service lines offered at a given facility.

tools.name: find_services
tools.title: Find Services
tools.description: Search H+H clinical service lines / specialties by category, facility, new-patient status, or free text.

tools.name: find_providers
tools.title: Find Providers
tools.description: Find a Doctor — search H+H clinicians by specialty, language, facility, or new-patient status.

tools.name: find_financial_assistance
tools.title: Find Financial Assistance
tools.description: List H+H financial-assistance and coverage programs — NYC Care, the sliding-scale (Options) fee schedule, and financial counseling. Use for uninsured or under-insured users; NYC Care and the sliding scale are available regardless of immigration status.

tools.name: find_pharmacies
tools.title: Find Pharmacies
tools.description: List H+H outpatient / retail pharmacies by borough or facility.

tools.name: list_my_appointments
tools.title: List My Appointments
tools.description: List the authenticated patient's booked appointments and their status — mirrors MyChart and the live Epic FHIR Appointment resource (read-only). Requires SMART on FHIR patient authorization.

tools.name: request_appointment
tools.title: Request an Appointment
tools.description: Request an appointment at NYC Health + Hospitals (net-new). NO public booking API exists today — patients call 1-844-NYC-4NYC or use a MyChart flow, and the live Epic FHIR surface exposes no Appointment create, no Slot, and no $book. Requires an authenticated patient. Mutates state — confirm service, facility, and patient details first; set urgent=true for time-sensitive but non-emergency needs; NEVER use for emergencies (direct to 911 / an Emergency Department). Route uninsured requesters to NYC Care / financial counseling via the coverage block.
